playwright.purpose: Project-scoped browser automation and UI verification for MCP-compatible clients.
onboarding: Open the project from a client that supports project-level .mcp.json.
Trust or approve the Playwright MCP server the first time your client prompts.
Use qa-simulator or frontend workflows that rely on Playwright telemetry.

drawio.purpose: Diagram generation and draw.io-compatible MCP tooling for architecture and docs.
onboarding: Approve the drawio MCP server in your client when prompted.
Use it for architecture diagrams when Mermaid-only output is not enough.

figma.purpose: Figma integration for design extraction and token workflows.
onboarding: Replace the FIGMA_ACCESS_TOKEN placeholder in .mcp.json or client MCP config.
Keep the token out of git if you move it to a user-level config.
Treat Figma MCP as optional; missing token must not block the rest of the stack.

stitch.purpose: Custom Stitch MCP integration when the downstream package is available in the client environment.
onboarding: Approve the Stitch MCP server in your client if you use Stitch flows.
If the package is unavailable in your environment, leave it optional and continue with core MCP servers.
